name: Suffolk (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the gold of the crown of King Edmund the Martyr

description: A blue field with two gold crowns of St Edmund above, and a single arrow piercing a crown below.
history: Adopted in 2017 after a public competition organised by the Suffolk Society and Suffolk County Council; registered with the Flag Institute on 16 August 2017.

features.symbolism: King Edmund the Martyr, patron saint of Suffolk and former patron of England
St Edmund's martyrdom by the Danes in 869
